metadata.title: Guideline on clinical investigation of medicinal products for prevention of venous thromboembolism (VTE) in non-surgical patients

rules.rule_text: The primary efficacy outcome in Phase III confirmatory trials for VTE prophylaxis in non-surgical patients must be a composite of: documented asymptomatic proximal DVT, documented symptomatic DVT (proximal and distal), documented symptomatic non-fatal PE, and VTE-related death (non-inferiority trials) or all-cause death (superiority trials).
rules.verbatim_quote: The main efficacy outcome recommended in Phase III trials in the prophylaxis of VTE in non-surgical patients is the composite of: Documented asymptomatic proximal DVT, Documented symptomatic DVT (proximal and distal), Documented symptomatic non-fatal PE, VTE-related death (non-inferiority trials) or all-cause death (superiority trials)

rules.rule_text: In non-inferiority trials, VTE-related death (or a death considered to be due to VTE, such as fatal PE and sudden death) is recommended as part of the composite endpoint. In superiority trials, death from any cause is recommended.
rules.verbatim_quote: In non-inferiority trials, it is generally recommended to choose an endpoint reflecting as much as possible the effect of a drug; therefore, a VTE-related death (or a death considered to be due to VTE, such as fatal PE and sudden death, as autopsy findings may not be always available) is recommended as part of a composite endpoint. For superiority trials, death from any cause is recommended as part of a composite endpoint.

rules.rule_text: A supportive analysis of the composite endpoint must be provided using the alternative group of deaths (VTE-related death for superiority trial, all-cause death for non-inferiority trial).
rules.verbatim_quote: In both cases, a supportive analysis of the composite endpoint should be provided using the alternative group of deaths i.e. VTE-related death for a superiority trial and all-cause death for a non-inferiority trial.

rules.rule_text: All major endpoints must be adjudicated by a blinded clinical events committee.
rules.verbatim_quote: All major endpoints should be adjudicated by a blinded clinical events committee.

rules.rule_text: Mandatory routine bilateral lower extremity CUS must be performed after the last dose of study medication or matching placebo. In subjects prematurely discontinuing or developing DVT symptoms, bilateral CUS must be performed at that time and at the initially programmed day of end of treatment.
rules.verbatim_quote: Mandatory routine bilateral lower extremity CUS should be performed after the last dose of study medication or matching placebo. In subjects prematurely discontinuing their treatment and/or developing symptoms of DVT, bilateral CUS should be performed at that time and at the initially programmed day of end of treatment.

rules.rule_text: Diagnosis of symptomatic DVT or PE based solely on clinical signs and symptoms is inadequate and discouraged. Objective testing is required.
rules.verbatim_quote: Diagnosis of symptomatic DVT or PE based solely on clinical signs and symptoms is inadequate and therefore is discouraged.

rules.rule_text: The primary efficacy endpoint must be investigated within a follow-up period after trial drug discontinuation, usually 30 days, to rule out a potential rebound effect.
rules.verbatim_quote: The primary efficacy endpoint should also be investigated within a follow-up period after trial drug discontinuation, usually 30 days, in order to rule out a potential rebound effect.
rules.exceptions: For patients with permanent/persistent VTE risk factors, additional follow-up efficacy assessment (3 to 6 months after withdrawal of prophylaxis) may also be discussed.

rules.rule_text: A mandatory secondary analysis must include the individual components of the recommended primary efficacy endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: A mandatory secondary analysis should include the individual components of the recommended primary efficacy endpoint.

rules.rule_text: Deaths must be carefully characterised regarding their relationship to VTE, according to criteria specified in the study protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: Deaths should be carefully characterised regarding their relationship to VTE, according to criteria specified in the study protocol.

rules.rule_text: In cases of suspected fatal PE, effort must be made to obtain an autopsy report to confirm the diagnosis.
rules.verbatim_quote: In cases of 'suspected fatal PE,' effort should be made to obtain an autopsy report to confirm the diagnosis. Unless PE has been excluded, it will be difficult to attribute any death to a non-PE cause.

rules.rule_text: It is recommended to use the same methods for diagnosis of VTE across all trials. Bilateral CUS, ascending venography, spiral CT, pulmonary angiography, and ventilation-perfusion lung scan are considered acceptable diagnostic methods.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended to use the same methods for diagnosis of VTE across all the trials. The following diagnostic methods are considered acceptable for documenting DVT and PE.

rules.rule_text: All sonographers performing CUS must receive CUS training to ensure high quality of standardised examinations. Video recordings of CUS can be adjudicated centrally.
rules.verbatim_quote: Video recordings of CUS examinations can be adjudicated centrally, but all sonographers have to receive CUS training to ensure a high quality of standardized CUS, particularly if a quantitative evaluation of thrombus burden is to be conducted.

rules.rule_text: Screening proximal veins at baseline with CUS to detect old thrombi should be considered to increase diagnostic precision.
rules.verbatim_quote: The possibility of screening the proximal veins at baseline with CUS to detect old thrombi should be considered in order to increase its diagnostic precision.

rules.rule_text: Patients must be selected on the basis of target population and intended indication. The trial population must be representative of acutely-ill medical patients at high thromboembolic risk.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients should be selected on the basis of target population and intended indication. ... It is important that the trial population would be representative of acutely-ill medical patients at high thromboembolic risk.
rules.exceptions: In certain cases, if scientifically justified, the indication may be restricted to a more limited population.

rules.rule_text: Patients must be included at high risk of thrombosis as assessed by available risk scores (Padua Score, Improve Score, Geneva score). In addition to immobilisation or reduced mobility, there should preferably be at least one additional risk factor present.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended to include patients at high risk of thrombosis, as assessed by available risk scores in hospitalized medical patients, like the Padua Score [12], Improve Score [14] and Geneva score [15]. In addition to immobilisation or reduced mobility, there should be preferably at least one additional risk factor present.

rules.rule_text: If an indication for VTE prophylaxis is sought in a non-acute setting (e.g. outpatients with cancer receiving chemotherapy, non-acutely ill patients with prolonged immobilisation), inclusion of high-risk patients as assessed by validated scores in separate studies is recommended.
rules.verbatim_quote: If an indication for VTE prophylaxis is sought in a non-acute setting, like for example, for thromboprophylaxis in outpatients with cancer receiving chemotherapy [6,7], or in non acutely ill medical patients with prolonged immobilisation, the inclusion of high-risk patients as assessed by validated scores in separate studies is recommended.

rules.rule_text: Stratified randomisation may be needed to account for prognostic risk factors that may significantly influence the primary outcome.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is important that the trial population is reflective of the variety of predisposing risk factors. Stratified randomisation may be needed to account for prognostic risk factors that may significantly influence the primary outcome.

rules.rule_text: Confirmatory trials must use a prospective, double-blind, randomised, controlled, parallel group design. If blinding is not possible, the trial must still be controlled and randomised with independent adjudication committees for efficacy and safety.
rules.verbatim_quote: For confirmatory trials a prospective, double-blind randomised, controlled, parallel group clinical trial is recommended. Even if blinding is not possible, the trial should be controlled and randomised. In such trials, evaluation of efficacy and safety should be carried out by independent adjudication committees.

rules.rule_text: An appropriate follow-up of at least 30 days after treatment discontinuation must be included to assess a possible rebound effect.
rules.verbatim_quote: An appropriate follow-up of at least 30 days after treatment discontinuation should be included to assess a possible rebound effect.

rules.rule_text: If a medicinal product is already approved or recommended in treatment guidelines, an active treatment must be included in the study design. Placebo-controlled design may be justified only if patients are followed with repeated US investigations at regular intervals and no prophylactic method is registered in the targeted indication.
rules.source_section: 6.4 Therapeutic confirmatory studies - Choice of comparator
rules.verbatim_quote: If a medicinal product is already approved or recommended in treatment guidelines, an active treatment should be included in the study design, otherwise it should be fully justified. A placebo-controlled design could in some situations be justified if the patients are followed with repeated US investigations at regular intervals. In situations when no prophylactic methods have yet been registered in the targeted indication, superiority over placebo should be demonstrated.

rules.rule_text: For acute medical illness with reduced mobility/immobilisation, treatment must be administered at least until full mobilisation prior to discharge, usually 7-14 days.
rules.source_section: 6.4 Therapeutic confirmatory studies - Duration of treatment
rules.verbatim_quote: In the case of acute medical illness, when patients have reduced mobility or are immobilised, treatment should be administered at least until full mobilisation prior to discharge. The duration of treatment in this situation is usually for 7-14 days.

rules.rule_text: In long-term thromboprophylaxis studies, the protocol must describe the management of anticoagulant/antithrombotic therapy during elective and urgent surgical procedures and in major trauma.
rules.verbatim_quote: Concomitant procedures: in long-term thromboprophylaxis studies, the protocol has to describe the management of anticoagulant/antithrombotic therapy during the clinical trial in case study subjects have to undergo elective and urgent surgical procedures as well as in the event of major trauma.

rules.rule_text: Non-inferiority testing (followed by superiority if non-inferiority is demonstrated) is the recommended approach in active controlled trials. The non-inferiority margin must be pre-specified and justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: Non-inferiority testing (followed by superiority if non-inferiority is demonstrated) is the recommended approach in active controlled trials. The analysis of non-inferiority and superiority should follow general statistical guidelines (ICH E9). In non-inferiority trials, the choice of the non-inferiority margin should be pre-specified and justified (CPMP/EWP/2158/99).

rules.rule_text: On-treatment analyses and analyses including events occurring between 7 and 30 days after study drug discontinuation must be conducted to investigate possible early rebound increase in thromboembolism.
rules.verbatim_quote: 'On-treatment' analyses and analyses including events occurring between 7 days and 30 days after study drug discontinuation can be conducted in order to investigate a possible early rebound increase in thromboembolism after treatment cessation.

rules.rule_text: PK profile must be studied in patients with impaired renal function, impaired liver function, extreme body-weights, and older patients.
rules.verbatim_quote: the pharmacokinetic profile of the product in development should also be studied in the following specific patient populations: patients with impaired renal function, impaired liver function, extreme body-weights, and older patients.

rules.rule_text: Patients >65 years and >=75 years must be appropriately represented in clinical trials. Clinical data in older persons (>=75) and the oldest group (>=85 years) with high comorbidity is of utmost importance.
rules.source_section: 6.5 Studies in special populations - Older patients
rules.verbatim_quote: to assess the benefit/risk balance of a drug that will be used in the geriatric population, patients >65 years and >=75 years should be appropriately represented in clinical trials (ICH E7 and Clinical Trials Regulation 536/2014, art 6). ... Generating clinical data in older persons (>=75) and also in the oldest group (>=85 years) of patients with high comorbidity is a matter of utmost importance.

rules.rule_text: The primary safety endpoint in pivotal trials must be major bleeding. The sum of major and clinically relevant non-major bleeding must be analysed as a secondary endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: In pivotal trials, the recommended primary safety endpoint is major bleeding, but the sum of major and clinically relevant non-major bleeding is to be analysed as well (secondary endpoint).
rules.exceptions: In dose-finding studies, sum of major and clinically relevant non-major bleeding is recommended.

rules.rule_text: Major bleeding is defined as: fatal bleeding; critical organ bleeding (intracranial, intraocular, intraspinal, pericardial, retroperitoneal, intraarticular or intramuscular with compartment syndrome); clinically overt bleeding with haemoglobin decrease >2 g/dL; transfusion of >=2 units blood/packed cells; or clinically overt bleeding necessitating surgical intervention.
rules.verbatim_quote: Major bleeding is defined as a bleeding event that meets at least one of the following criteria: fatal bleeding; critical bleeding (intracranial, intraocular, intraspinal, pericardial, retroperitoneal, intraarticular or intramuscular with compartment syndrome); clinically overt bleeding associated with a decrease in the haemoglobin level of more than 2 g/dL (20 g/l; 1.24 mmol/L) compared with the pre-randomisation level; clinically overt bleeding leading to transfusion of two or more units of whole blood or packed cells; clinically overt bleeding that necessitates surgical intervention

rules.rule_text: The adjudication of bleeding events by a central independent and blinded committee of experts, using pre-specified limits and clear terms of reference, is strongly encouraged.
rules.verbatim_quote: Similar to the efficacy evaluation, the adjudication of bleeding events by a central independent and blinded committee of experts, using pre-specified limits and clear terms of reference is strongly encouraged.

rules.rule_text: For heparin-class parenteral anticoagulants, thrombocytopenia and injection site hematomas must be included as secondary safety outcomes.
rules.verbatim_quote: In studies including a parenteral anticoagulant of the heparin class, the inclusion of thrombocytopenia and injection sites hematomas as secondary safety outcomes is also important.

rules.rule_text: If there is potential for DILI with the study drugs, an algorithm for hepatic monitoring must be included in the protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: If there is a potential for drug-induced liver injury (DILI) with the study drugs (experimental and/or control), an algorithm for hepatic monitoring has to be included in the protocol.

rules.rule_text: Development of a specific antidote or reversal agent studies and a standardised test for laboratory monitoring of anticoagulant effect are highly recommended for new agents.
rules.source_section: 8.1 The need for reversal and laboratory monitoring
rules.verbatim_quote: The development of a specific antidote or further specific studies with non-specific reversal agent for new antithrombotics when given at high doses for long-term is highly recommended given the potential for life-threatening bleeding events in standard practice. ... The development of a standardised test for laboratory monitoring of the anticoagulant effect of new agents is highly recommended.
